atoms.semantic_claim: Async commitments require configured state and EVM support, unique economic senders per block, no active duplicate job, structural validation, wallet/lock checks except heartbeat commitments, two-phase deadline offset bounds, and parent-state EVM re-simulation.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves commitment validity and builder/verifier parity checks.

atoms.semantic_claim: Scheduled transactions are verified from deterministic Scheduler and Heartbeat parent state: heartbeat CH2-CH9 checks, Scheduler C1-C10 via shared verifier, and C11 predicate execution with per-predicate and per-block gas limits.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 1903-2468 preserve scheduled transaction collection, heartbeat validation, shared C1-C10 verification, and C11 predicate evaluation exactly.
Payload builder contains corresponding C11 obligation filtering behavior used as bounded parity evidence.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves deterministic parent-state scheduled validity rules.

atoms.semantic_claim: Nonce-lock verification requires parent state and delegates to `crate::nonce_lock::verify_nonce_locks`, preserving the shared duplicate and in-flight async sender checks.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves the verifier-to-helper boundary and parent-state source.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: An ordinary transaction from a sender with an active async job should reject through the delegated nonce-lock path.

atoms.semantic_claim: The verifier extracts at most one allowlisted `spc_calls` and `delivery_spc_calls` attachment per transaction, rejects malformed proofs and executor addresses, reads jobs and executor keys from parent-state contracts, checks commit block and input hash, and verifies executor JSON signatures.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 2516-2923 preserve attachment extraction, allowlist checks, job/key reads, input hash checks, and signature verification exactly.
Destination payload builder has corresponding delivery SPC call handling on the builder path.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves cryptographic verification and fail-closed malformed attachment handling.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Delivery SPC without `origin_tx_hash`, short proof, zero executor, bad input hash, or invalid signature should reject.

atoms.source_snippet:
fn verify_sequencing_rights(...) -> Result<(), BlockVerificationError> { ... }
// static/top-level/full-call-graph classification and priority monotonicity per contract
atoms.semantic_claim: Sequencing rights verification requires parent state/header and EVM config, uses the same static, top-level, and full-call-graph classification and priority computation as the builder, and rejects descending priority within each contract bucket.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 2925-3146 preserve transaction classification and monotonic priority enforcement per sequencing-rights contract.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves ordering semantics and builder-shared sequencing-rights components.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Two transactions for the same sequencing-rights contract ordered with lower priority after higher priority should reject.

atoms.semantic_claim: The verifier recomputes scheduled obligations from parent state, filters them by C11 predicates using builder-shared logic, builds fulfilled sets from scheduled transactions and async commitments wrapping scheduled origins, excludes obligations with active async jobs, and rejects omitted obligations when block gas capacity remains.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 3148-3395 preserve obligation computation, predicate evaluation, fulfilled scanning, active-job exclusion, and gas-capacity rejection.
Destination builder path shares scheduled predicate filtering logic with the verifier.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves censorship-detection semantics and active-job exclusion.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A due obligation omitted from a block with enough remaining gas and no active async job should reject.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds ABI helper functions that parse delivery result length and bytes, agent metrics, U256-sized integers, and settlement `precompileInput` for settle and deliver selectors with bounds checks.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 3425-3683 preserve selector dispatch and ABI offset/length guards exactly.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves parsing and malformed-calldata rejection behavior.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Calldata with unknown selector, out-of-bounds offset, or truncated dynamic bytes should return an error or `None` as appropriate.
